title: Hypothesis Verification Gate Entry
description: Tracks the verification status of a root-cause hypothesis in an SEO recovery operation. Live-fix actions are only permitted when status is 'verified' or 'fixed'. See references/HYPOTHESIS_VERIFICATION_GATE.md for the full concept.

hypothesis_id.description: Stable identifier for this hypothesis entry. Format: hvg-<short-slug>.

hypothesis_status.description: Current verification status. Live fixes are only permitted at 'verified' or 'fixed'.

hypothesis.description: Concise statement of the suspected cause and mechanism. Should answer 'what produces the observed effect, by what mechanism'.

observed_effect.description: What is the actual symptom being explained? Should be measurable and grounded in real data.

source.description: Verification source type. See HYPOTHESIS_VERIFICATION_GATE.md for the strength hierarchy.

observation.description: What was observed from this source that supports the hypothesis.

strength.description: Verification strength tier. weak sources alone cannot move a hypothesis to 'verified'.

captured_by.description: Who or what produced this evidence (e.g., 'ai_diagnose', 'operator', 'developer_name', 'crawl_id').

artifact_path.description: Optional path to the underlying artifact (crawl export, HTML snapshot, API response).

resolution.description: How was this contradiction handled? Options: 'hypothesis_refined', 'hypothesis_status_reset_to_suspected', 'evidence_explained_as_artifact', 'hypothesis_closed'.

contradicting_evidence.description: Evidence that contradicts the hypothesis. A non-empty entry should either resolve the contradiction or downgrade the hypothesis status.

source_of_truth_checked.description: Which source-of-truth verification categories have been performed. For 'verified' status, at least one entry must be from the strong or strongest tier (api, server_file, gsc, staging, developer_inspect, operator_inspect).

excluded_by.description: What evidence ruled out this alternative.

excluded_alternatives.description: Alternative hypotheses that were considered and ruled out. For 'verified' status, this array must be non-empty unless explicitly justified.

verified_by.description: Single-value summary of the strongest verification source used. 'not_yet_verified' is the only valid value when hypothesis_status is 'suspected' or 'likely'.

live_fix_allowed.description: Computed field: must be true only when hypothesis_status is 'verified' or 'fixed'. Any other status forces false.

affected_urls.description: URLs in scope for the verified fix. Expanding beyond this list requires re-verification of the expanded scope.

affected_components.description: Code components, plugins, templates, or configuration sections in scope.

minimal_change_description.description: The smallest reversible change that addresses the verified cause.

fix_scope.description: Only required when hypothesis_status is 'verified' or beyond. Defines the bounds of the permitted live fix.

approval_scope_match.description: True if the operator's approval explicitly matches the verified fix_scope. Required for live deploy.

operator_approval.description: Required when hypothesis_status is 'fixed' or beyond. Operator approval must reference the verified cause and the fix_scope.

settlement_gate_override.description: If the verified fix was deployed during an active Settlement Gate, this object documents the override decision.

status_history.description: Audit trail of status transitions. Required for any hypothesis that reaches 'verified' or beyond.
